pi_items.source: task.json description line 5, setup_task.sh lines 39-42, export_result.sh lines 18-47 - all consistently reference field_related_accessories

seed_products.php line 46 defines: ["title" => "Apple MacBook Air M2 13-inch", ...]. Confirmed as the only Apple laptop product in database. Cross-referenced with create_featured_products_block/validated_pi.json which identifies same contradiction.
seed_products.php line 49 defines: ["title" => "Logitech MX Master 3S Wireless Mouse", ...]. Exact title match required by get_product_id_by_title() function in task_utils.sh lines 94-97.
seed_products.php line 45 defines: ["title" => "Sony WH-1000XM5 Wireless Headphones", ...]. Exact title match required by get_product_id_by_title() function in task_utils.sh lines 94-97.

privileged_info_summary: The Drupal Commerce environment contains 12 seeded products generated by seed_products.php. The main product referenced in task instructions, 'Apple MacBook Pro 16"', does not exist in the database; the actual product is 'Apple MacBook Air M2 13-inch' (line 46 of seed_products.php). The two accessory products exist but with full descriptive titles: 'Logitech MX Master 3S Wireless Mouse' (not just 'Logitech MX Master 3S') and 'Sony WH-1000XM5 Wireless Headphones' (not just 'Sony WH-1000XM5'). The expected field name 'field_related_accessories' is correctly specified. The export_result.sh verifier script uses case-insensitive exact title matching (task_utils.sh lines 94-97) and will fail to locate the main product and accessory products due to these title mismatches.
